stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant large language model.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its community relevance and performance tracking.
The associated tags confirm its relevance to transformer architectures and text generation tasks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The tags confirm its relevance to the broader AI/ML ecosystem, specifically involving transformers and Llama architectures.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components such as transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on practical model deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is text-generation, indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.
The tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern deep learning ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, enhancing local AI inference speed on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview release, indicating ongoing development and adoption.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Small modular nuclear reactor reaches criticality in first test
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: A small modular nuclear reactor (SMR) has achieved criticality in its initial test.
The technology originates from a startup named Antares.
The reactor is not yet operational for power generation.

stories.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: S&P 500 is reportedly blocking SpaceX from gaining easy access to passive investor capital.
The exclusion also reportedly affects major AI players like OpenAI and Anthropic.
This suggests a growing divergence or scrutiny regarding how high-growth, often private, tech companies are integrated into traditional investment indices.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Code2LoRA introduces a hypernetwork framework to generate repository-specific LoRA adapters for code language models.
This method addresses the limitations of current techniques (long inputs/per-repository fine-tuning) by injecting repository knowledge without increasing inference-time token overhead.
It aims to provide code models with necessary repository-level context (imports, APIs, conventions) while remaining robust to software evolution.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a novel LLM-based framework designed for self-evolving Automated Machine Learning (MLE).
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, specifically inter-branch information isolation, memoryless search, and poor hierarchical control.
Aims to enable sustained, long-horizon optimization for complex tasks like scientific discovery and algorithm design.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Introduces Code2LoRA, a hypernetwork framework designed to inject repository-level context into Code Language Models.
Addresses the limitations of current methods (long input context or full fine-tuning) which are costly and brittle to code evolution.
Achieves repository-specific knowledge injection without incurring any inference-time token overhead.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a novel LLM-based framework designed for self-evolving Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) algorithm discovery.
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, such as inter-branch information isolation, memoryless search, and lack of hierarchical control.
Positions self-evolving capabilities as a key requirement for LLM agents tackling complex, long-horizon tasks like scientific discovery.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon devices.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run entirely on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local large language models.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining user data privacy by running locally.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has expanded its capabilities to support the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
The update facilitates the use of proprietary/advanced models alongside open-source models.

stories.title: Meta made its own AI-generated clickbait news feed
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Meta is leveraging AI to generate its own clickbait-style news content.
The new feature is integrated into the standalone Meta AI app's "For You" section.
The AI generates the topics, images, and text for these questionable articles.

stories.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A USB-connected speaker has been shown to potentially infect a PC without physical user interaction.
The specific device mentioned is the Sound Blaster Katana V2X.
The manufacturer does not classify this behavior as a security vulnerability.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Introduces HANDOFF, a new method for whole-body control in humanoid robots.
Addresses the critical challenge of bridging high-level task planning with low-level robot control.
Proposes a compact, explicit, and intuitive interface to replace complex kinematic references.

stories.title: TempoVLA: Learning Speed-Controllable Vision-Language-Action Policies
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: TempoVLA addresses a key limitation in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models: the inability to control execution speed dynamically.
The research tackles the need for robots to switch between fast transit phases and slow, precise contact stages during manipulation.
Unlike prior methods that only change between fixed speeds, TempoVLA specifically explores and enables controlled deceleration.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks by focusing on the co-editing process rather than just final outputs.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during human-AI text transformation.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into professional drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), specifically sequential processing and gradient issues.
It introduces Supervised Memory Training (SMT), a novel method designed to circumvent the need for recurrent credit propagation during RNN training.
SMT aims to reduce RNN training to a non-recurrent process, potentially improving parallelism and long-range dependency learning.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning of reasoning language models.
It proposes RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve RL training for Chain-of-Thought (CoT) traces.
This work targets limitations in existing methods like Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) which rely on Monte Carlo-style delayed rewards.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a method to leverage discarded, low-confidence tokens from discrete diffusion language models.
These discarded tokens serve as a valuable 'lookahead signal' for improving retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
The technique enhances the ability of models to retrieve stronger evidence by analyzing the early stages of the denoising process.

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method allows for merging preconditioned weights back into the original architecture with zero inference overhead.

stories.title: TailLoR: Protecting Principal Components in Parameter-Efficient Continual Learning
stories.author: Florin Brad
bullet_points: Introduces TailLoR, a new method for Parameter-Efficient Continual Learning (PECL).
TailLoR uses the singular bases (U and V) of pre-trained weights as a fixed reference frame.
It employs a soft spectral penalty to prevent updates from interfering with dominant singular directions, focusing adaptation on 'long-tail' components.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Addresses a critical challenge in humanoid robotics: bridging high-level task planning with low-level whole-body control.
Introduces HANDOFF, a novel, compact, and explicit interface designed to be intuitive and general for diverse manipulation skills.
Uses a method involving Distilled Complementary Teachers to achieve effective control, suggesting an advanced ML approach to robotics.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks, which fail to account for documents resulting from progressive human-AI co-editing.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during the revision process.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into real-world drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), specifically regarding sequential computation and gradient issues.
It introduces Supervised Memory Training (SMT), a novel method designed to train nonlinear RNNs.
SMT bypasses the need for traditional recurrent credit propagation, potentially improving parallelism and long-range dependency learning.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning of reasoning language models.
It introduces RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve RL training for models generating Chain-of-Thought (CoT) traces.
This work targets limitations of standard Monte Carlo methods (like GRPO) when rewards are only available after the entire reasoning process is complete.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a novel method, Self-Augmenting Retrieval, for Diffusion Language Models (DLMs).
DLMs generate text by iteratively denoising the entire response in parallel, predicting tentative tokens at each step.
The discarded, low-confidence tokens from the denoising process are shown to be a valuable lookahead signal for improving retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
It offers performance benefits over standard transformers without adding inference overhead after training.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks by focusing on the progressive nature of human-AI co-editing.
Introduces 'OpAI-Bench,' a new operation-guided benchmark designed to track how AI authorship signals evolve during revision.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into professional drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a method to leverage 'discarded' low-confidence tokens from discrete diffusion language models.
These discarded tokens are shown to act as a valuable 'lookahead signal' for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
This suggests a novel way to improve the retrieval process by analyzing the intermediate steps of text generation.

stories.title: You Only Index Once: Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing
stories.author: Furu Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses the efficiency bottleneck in long-context inference for LLMs, particularly during complex reasoning tasks.
It tackles the common trade-off between speed and quality in existing sparse attention methods.
The proposed 'Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing' aims to improve both decoding efficiency and model accuracy.

stories.title: Scaffold, Not Vocabulary? A Controlled, Two-Tier, Pre-Registered Study of a Popperian Code-Generation Skill
stories.author: Mehmet Iscan
bullet_points: The study investigates the efficacy of 'Popperian' prompting techniques to improve code generation by Large Language Models (LLMs).
It critically examines the reliance on LLM-as-a-judge systems, noting their inherent biases (positional, self-preference, stylistic).
The research questions whether observed performance gains from these prompting skills are genuine or artifacts of the biased evaluation methods.

stories.title: Latent Reasoning with Normalizing Flows
stories.author: Jiatao Gu
bullet_points: The paper proposes 'Latent Reasoning' as an alternative to explicit Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting in LLMs.
Traditional textual CoT forces reasoning through a discrete, serial token stream, which limits computational bandwidth.
Latent reasoning aims to perform intermediate computation in a higher-bandwidth manner, potentially capturing semantic or uncertain updates more effectively.

stories.title: USAD 2.0: Scaling Representation Distillation for Universal Audio Understanding
stories.author: James Glass
bullet_points: Introduces USAD 2.0, a new universal audio encoder designed to handle diverse audio inputs.
Addresses the limitation of current domain-specific audio encoders by integrating knowledge from both Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) and supervised methods.
Highlights the growing trend of LLMs relying on unified encoders for various input modalities, including audio.

stories.title: Revising Context, Shifting Simulated Stance: Auditing LLM-Based Stance Simulation in Online Discussions
stories.author: Jiebo Luo
bullet_points: The research addresses the reliability of using LLMs to simulate online user stances in discussions.
It proposes 'counterfactual context revision' as a method to audit how sensitive these LLM simulations are to context changes.
The study investigates whether LLM simulations accurately reflect genuine user beliefs or are merely artifacts of conversational phrasing.

stories.title: The Smart TV in Your LivingRoom Is a Node in the AIScraping Economy
stories.author: blog.includesecurity.com via gerikson
bullet_points: Smart TVs are being analyzed as nodes within the 'AIScraping Economy'.
The article suggests consumer devices are integral to data collection for AI systems.
This points to a growing concern over data privacy and surveillance in smart home technology.

stories.title: Run Coding Agents on Local AI — Zero Cloud, Full Control
stories.author: dalenguyen
bullet_points: The article discusses the growing utility of coding agents (e.g., Codex CLI, Claude Code, Cursor, Pi) as productivity tools.
The core theme is the shift towards running these coding agents locally, eliminating reliance on cloud services.
This local execution promises users 'Zero Cloud, Full Control' over their AI development workflow.

stories.title: Teaching a Reranker the Language of Security Tickets (+41% MRR@10)
stories.author: vinayiitkgp
bullet_points: The article details using a RAG pipeline to process a large volume of historical security data (142,000+ closed XSOAR tickets).
The core focus is on 'teaching a Reranker the language of security tickets,' suggesting advanced NLP/ML application in Security Operations Centers (SOCs).
The implementation resulted in a measurable business impact, specifically a +41% increase in Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR@10).

stories.title: SOC-in-a-Box: One LLM, Eight Hats, A Production-Bar AI SOC on a Single GPU
stories.author: vinayiitkgp
bullet_points: Introduces 'SOC-in-a-Box,' a concept for a production-ready Security Operations Center (SOC) powered by a single LLM.
The system simulates multiple specialized roles (eight 'hats') typically required in a real SOC, such as alert triage.
The claim suggests this complex, multi-role AI system can operate efficiently on a single GPU.
